reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider tesamorelin first when the indication is visceral / abdominal fat in someone who can tolerate a stronger GHRH analog: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:06:03.880) says "tesamorelin is a far stronger option," citing "15 to 18 18% reductions in trunk fat for over uh, 26 weeks" (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:06:08.080), and Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:18:34.280) says "Tesamorelin, you can go right up to 2 milligrams a night. You don't have to, you start at one." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh leaner female patients separately because Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:20:38.800) says "women that are leaner don't seem to like tesamorelin as much because it's so strong, and so they feel like they get bloated," while Taylor's 2024 women's protocol (pyNE59aea-o 00:11:33.560) frames it as "1 milligram" PM in the 20-25% body fat range with a 6-week (max 8) cycle.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Water retention / bloat: Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:05:08.800): "it can cause you to retain water and make you look a little bit more bloated"; Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:09:44.600): "chances are you're going to get tired of that bloat look so eight is or six is going to be a long"; Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:20:42.000): "women that are leaner don't seem to like tesamorelin as much because it's so strong, and so they feel like they get bloated."
- Cycle, don't run forever: Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:08:59.800): "you can't stay on these for a while you have to do Cycles with them"; Hunter 2023 baseline_protocol lists "Duration" "8 weeks on, 8 weeks off" with "Frequency" "5 days on, 2 days off."
- Subq injection — oral does not work for GHRH analogs: Trevor (HE1Dre7T0Fs 00:04:20.000): "Anybody selling a single oral peptide? A single one. Put them on live with me. I'll set your hair on fire. Absolutely not. They do not work."
- HGH outranks tesamorelin in 55+ longevity context: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:17:05.079): "HGH, especially for someone that's 55, is always going to be better"; Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:17:13.560) says Ipa+Tessa "are not worthless by any means" — Toyota 4Runner vs Ferrari analogy.
- Insulin/glucose sensitivity warning re: exogenous HGH (NOT tesamorelin): Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:05:15.919) says exogenous GH "create insulin resistance and you swell your carpal tunnels in your wrists," framing tesamorelin as preferable because Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:05:34.360) says "Tesamorelin is the signal for growth hormone" — i.e., it preserves negative-feedback loops.
- Sermorelin is NOT a substitute: Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:14:35.440): "sermorelin? Forget it. Sermorelin is a 29 amino acid fragment. It is the obsolete eight-track player stereotype"; Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:14:43.600): "Tesamorelin is the finished product, superior stability, longer half-life and proven clinical superiority in elevating IGF levels."
- Ovarian-health claim is unverified: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:20:00.480): "I do think tesamorelin could be beneficial um for ovarian health, although I don't know specifically if there's research."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Quoted overlap on visceral / belly-fat indication: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:05:59.960): "I would use tesamorelin and or a GLP-1 agonist. So, if the goal is targeted fat loss, tesamorelin is a far stronger option"; Hunter email threadId 19621e264ba38f4e (2025-04-10): "Clinically approved to reduce stubborn belly fat (particularly visceral fat — the dangerous stuff that wraps around your organs), Tesamorelin powerfully stimulates your pituitary gland to naturally release more growth hormone"; Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:04:54.320): "Tessa marelin I do see a huge benefit with losing the belly fat"; Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:08:50.920): "this is more my peptide go-to for men"; Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:05:34.360): "Tesamorelin is the signal for growth hormone" and (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:14:43.600): "Tesamorelin is the finished product, superior stability, longer half-life." Quoted overlap on subq route: Hunter email threadId 19621e264ba38f4e (2025-04-10): "Inject subcutaneously every night before bed, ideally on an empty stomach"; Taylor's standard "fasted" subq frame at AM or PM (pyNE59aea-o 00:11:42.480): "you're going to take it fasted"; Trevor (HE1Dre7T0Fs 00:04:25.480): oral peptides "do not work." Cycling consensus: Hunter 2023 baseline_protocol "Duration" "8 weeks on, 8 weeks off"; Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:09:23.800): "do that for you know six weeks you can do it up to eight um I say do it for six."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Dose differs by an order of magnitude. Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:18:34.280): "Tesamorelin, you can go right up to 2 milligrams a night. You don't have to, you start at one, but don't be cheap" and (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:18:48.560): "Tesamorelin, 2 milligrams." Hunter 2023 baseline_protocol lists "Dose" "1 mg" with "Timing" "AM/PM," "Frequency" "5 days on, 2 days off." Hunter's 2025 blend protocol (email threadId 19621e264ba38f4e, 2025-04-10) gives a different per-night number for a stack: "Draw up 20 units (600mcg of Tesamorelin and 200mcg of ipamorelin) (0.2ml) of the reconstituted solution into your insulin syringe. Inject subcutaneously every night before bed." Taylor's 2024 women's protocol is lower than Trevor's max: Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:11:33.560): "is going to be 1 milligram" for the 20-25% body fat range. Acute timing differs: Trevor frames a single nightly dose to sync stage-three NREM (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:14:08.320): "Ingesting tesamorelin at night syncs the entire repair protocol with your body's innate circadian biology." Hunter's 2023 baseline_protocol lists "AM/PM" timing; Hunter's 2025 blend instruction (threadId 19621e264ba38f4e, 2025-04-10) says "You can also inject 10 units AM and 10 units PM if you find that it keeps you up at night." Taylor (pyNE59aea-o 00:12:17.120): "take it more in the evening um just because it can help you sleep a little bit better," but also (pyNE59aea-o 00:12:39.240) "I suggest taking it at night or in the morning um it's really up to you and how your body works with it." Tesamorelin vs HGH ranking: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:17:05.079): "HGH, especially for someone that's 55, is always going to be better" while Trevor (Fj9_wLXso1c 00:05:09.480) frames exogenous HGH as "trying to fix a Rolex or this Richard Mille with a sledgehammer" — Trevor prefers the GHRH-signal route, Hunter ranks HGH above tesamorelin for older clients.
